Who We Are

A Company Built for
Agent Success

Legacy Life Financial Group is a Field Marketing Organization (FMO) — meaning we sit at the top of the distribution chain, giving our agents access to dozens of the nation's top-rated carriers, the highest available contract levels, and resources that independent agents simply can't access on their own.

As an FMO, we don't just recruit agents — we build them. From licensing support and carrier contracting to ongoing mentorship and lead systems, Legacy Life exists to make every agent on our platform more productive, more profitable, and more independent.

Term Life

Simple, high-demand coverage — easy to sell and essential for families at every income level.

Whole Life

Permanent protection with cash value that grows — a powerful tool for long-term client relationships.

IUL

Indexed Universal Life — tax-advantaged growth tied to market indexes with downside protection. A premium product with strong commissions.

Annuities

Retirement income solutions your clients can count on — and a strong source of recurring commissions for you.

Our Network

Partnered with the Nation's Top Carriers

As a Field Marketing Organization, Legacy Life Financial Group provides agents direct access to over 30 of the most competitive, highly-rated carriers in the industry — giving you the products and commissions you need to build a thriving business.

Why Legacy Life

Everything You Need to
Earn Big from Day One

01

$10K–$15K in Your First Month

New agents consistently earn $10,000–$15,000 in their first month. Get your systems dialed in and the momentum builds fast — months 3 through 6 average $30,000–$50,000 for committed producers. Top producers reach $500,000+ per month.

02

Free Leads to Start — Then Scale

We provide free leads when you're starting out so you can hit the ground running with zero out-of-pocket. As you grow and your income increases, we'll show you how to strategically invest in additional lead sources to scale your business even further.

03

Own Your Business

You're not just an employee — you're building your own book of business. Enjoy the freedom of entrepreneurship with the support of an established organization behind you.

04

Specialized Training

Our onboarding and ongoing training programs are built by top producers who've been where you are. From licensing to closing, we guide every step of your journey.

05

Uncapped Commissions

There's no ceiling here. The harder you work, the more you earn. Our commission structure is among the most competitive in the industry, with performance bonuses on top.

06

Build & Lead a Team

Ready to scale? Recruit and mentor your own agents, build a downline, and earn overrides. Legacy Life rewards leaders who lift others up.

Legal

Privacy Policy & Data Security

Your Privacy Is Protected

Legacy Life Financial Group is committed to maintaining the highest standards of data privacy and security in full compliance with all applicable federal and state regulations, including the Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act (GLBA), the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA) where applicable, and applicable state insurance privacy laws. We collect only the information necessary to facilitate insurance transactions, agent onboarding, and organizational operations.

Encryption & Data Security

All agent and client information collected through Legacy Life Financial Group platforms — including personal identification details, financial data, health information, and policy records — is protected using industry-standard 256-bit SSL/TLS encryption. Data is transmitted securely at all times and stored in encrypted environments accessible only to authorized personnel. We employ strict access controls, multi-factor authentication, and regular security audits to ensure the ongoing integrity of all data systems.

Prohibition on Data Sale — Legal Consequences

Legacy Life Financial Group does not sell, rent, lease, or trade any agent or client personal information to third parties under any circumstances. Any unauthorized disclosure, sale, or commercial exploitation of agent or client data — whether by employees, contractors, affiliated agents, or any third party — constitutes a material breach of our terms of engagement and applicable law.

Individuals found to have sold, disclosed without authorization, or otherwise misappropriated agent or client data will be subject to immediate termination of their contract, civil litigation for damages, and referral to the appropriate state and federal regulatory authorities. Violations may result in substantial civil penalties and criminal prosecution under applicable federal and state statutes.

Information We Collect

We collect information provided directly by agents and clients through our secure portal, application forms, and communication channels. This includes name, contact information, professional licensing details, financial information for commission processing, and policy-related data necessary for carrier contracting and client service. We do not collect unnecessary personal information and do not retain data beyond the period required by regulatory and business obligations.

Your Rights

Agents and clients have the right to request access to their personal information, request corrections to inaccurate data, and request deletion of data subject to regulatory retention requirements. To exercise any of these rights, contact our compliance team at [email protected].

This policy is effective as of January 1, 2026 and is reviewed annually to ensure continued compliance with evolving regulatory requirements. Legacy Life Financial Group reserves the right to update this policy with appropriate notice to affected parties. For questions or concerns, contact [email protected].

Agent Onboarding Agreements

The following agreements must be acknowledged before submitting to the onboarding pipeline.

1. Non-Disclosure & Non-Solicitation Agreement

LEGACY LIFE FINANCIAL GROUP — NON-DISCLOSURE & NON-SOLICITATION AGREEMENT

This Non-Disclosure and Non-Solicitation Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into as of the date signed below, by and between Legacy Life Financial Group, LLC ("LLFG" or "the Company") and the undersigned independent agent ("Agent").

1. Confidential Information

Agent acknowledges that during the course of their relationship with LLFG, they will have access to confidential and proprietary information, including but not limited to:

  1. Client leads, prospect lists, and customer data (names, contact information, policy details, financial information)
  2. Proprietary sales processes, scripts, training materials, and methodologies
  3. Commission structures, compensation plans, override schedules, and bonus programs
  4. Internal business systems, CRM data, portal access credentials, and technology platforms
  5. Team structures, agent rosters, hierarchy data, and organizational information
  6. Marketing strategies, carrier relationships, and partnership agreements
  7. Financial records, production reports, and internal performance metrics

2. Non-Disclosure Obligations

Agent agrees to hold all Confidential Information in strict confidence and shall not, without prior written consent of LLFG:

  1. Disclose any Confidential Information to any third party
  2. Use any Confidential Information for any purpose other than performing duties for LLFG
  3. Copy, reproduce, or distribute any Confidential Information
  4. Remove or transfer any Confidential Information from LLFG systems upon departure

3. Non-Solicitation of Agents

For a period of twenty-four (24) months following the termination of Agent's relationship with LLFG, for any reason, Agent shall not directly or indirectly:

  1. Solicit, recruit, or attempt to recruit any current LLFG agent, manager, executive, or independent contractor to leave LLFG or join another organization
  2. Encourage, induce, or facilitate the departure of any LLFG team member
  3. Hire or engage any individual who was an LLFG agent within the 12 months preceding Agent's departure
  4. Contact LLFG agents for the purpose of offering competing business opportunities

4. Non-Solicitation of Clients

For a period of twenty-four (24) months following departure, Agent shall not:

  1. Solicit, contact, or service any client whose relationship originated through LLFG
  2. Use LLFG client data to generate business for any competing organization
  3. Interfere with any existing LLFG client relationships

5. Regulatory Compliance

Agent agrees to comply with all applicable federal, state, and local laws, regulations, and licensing requirements, including insurance licensing regulations, anti-fraud statutes, privacy laws, and all carrier compliance guidelines.

6. Return of Materials

Upon termination, Agent shall immediately return all LLFG property, including documents, electronic files, portal credentials, marketing materials, and any copies thereof.

7. Remedies

Agent acknowledges that a breach would cause irreparable harm. LLFG shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief, specific performance, and monetary damages, including reasonable attorney's fees and legal costs.

8. Survival

The obligations set forth in this Agreement shall survive termination for the applicable periods specified herein.

2. Debt Rollup & Financial Responsibility Agreement

LEGACY LIFE FINANCIAL GROUP — DEBT ROLLUP & FINANCIAL RESPONSIBILITY AGREEMENT

This Debt Rollup and Financial Responsibility Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into as of the date signed below, by and between Legacy Life Financial Group, LLC ("LLFG") and the undersigned independent agent ("Agent").

1. Chargeback Responsibility

Agent acknowledges and agrees that they are personally responsible for all chargebacks resulting from policies written by Agent that lapse, cancel, or are returned within the applicable chargeback window (typically 9-12 months, as determined by each carrier). When an advanced commission is paid on a policy that subsequently lapses, the corresponding chargeback amount becomes a debt owed by the Agent to LLFG.

2. Debt Rollup Mechanism

Any unpaid chargeback balances will be aggregated into a "Debt Rollup" balance. This balance:

  1. Accrues from the date of each individual chargeback
  2. Will be deducted from future commission payments until fully repaid
  3. Must be repaid in full before any commission advances are resumed
  4. Carries forward across commission periods until resolved
  5. Commission levels may decrease up to a maximum of 10% from the agent's highest production month if current monthly production declines

3. Team-Level Debt Responsibility

For Agents holding a Manager role or above, the Debt Rollup balance may include chargebacks attributed to their downline agents when those agents are unable to cover their own chargeback obligations. The upline Manager's override commissions will be applied to cover any shortfall.

4. Obligation Upon Departure

Agent must resolve all outstanding Debt Rollup balances in full prior to or upon termination of their relationship with LLFG. Specifically:

  1. All outstanding chargeback debt must be paid in full before the Agent's final separation from LLFG
  2. LLFG reserves the right to withhold any final commission payments to offset outstanding debt
  3. If outstanding debt exceeds final commission payments, Agent agrees to remit the remaining balance within thirty (30) days of departure

5. Legal Repercussions for Unpaid Debt

Failure to satisfy outstanding Debt Rollup obligations will result in legal action. Agent acknowledges and agrees that:

  1. LLFG may pursue civil litigation to recover all unpaid debt balances
  2. Agent will be responsible for the full debt amount plus all reasonable attorney's fees, court costs, collection costs, and related legal expenses incurred by LLFG
  3. LLFG may report unpaid debts to collections agencies and credit bureaus
  4. Interest may accrue on unpaid balances at the maximum rate permitted by applicable law
  5. Agent waives any right to contest the validity of the debt arising from verified chargebacks

6. Acknowledgment of Financial Risk

Agent acknowledges that the insurance business involves inherent financial risk, including the possibility of policy lapses and resulting chargebacks. Agent accepts this risk and agrees to manage their book of business responsibly to minimize chargeback exposure.

7. Governing Law

This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the state in which the Agent is primarily licensed. Any disputes shall be resolved through binding arbitration or in the appropriate court of jurisdiction.

Electronic Signature

Witnessed by:

Carrier Portals & Tools

Direct links to your carrier agent portals, quoting tools, and SureLc contracting — all in one place.

🔗 SureLc — Contracting Platform

SureLc is the contracting platform used to submit and track your carrier appointments. Link your SureLc account for direct access to your contracting status and carrier appointments.

Don't have a SureLc account? Contact your upline or email [email protected] to get set up. Your contracting must be completed through SureLc before you can write business with any carrier.

Access quoting tools for your top carriers. Each link opens the carrier's agent quoting portal in a new tab.

Promotion Guide Commission Tiers & Advancement

Your commission level advances automatically as you hit monthly AP milestones. Each tier is reviewed monthly. Promotions to Manager, Executive, and Managing Partner include team override income and expanded carrier access.

How It Works

Commission levels advance automatically when you hit monthly AP milestones. Each tier is reviewed monthly based on your personal production. Promotion to Manager, Executive, and Managing Partner tiers includes team override income, expanded carrier contract access, and organizational leadership responsibilities.

Fill in the agent's details. Their @llfg.us business email is generated automatically. Clicking Create Account will simultaneously add them to the portal, send their credentials, and open Google Workspace Admin to create their company Gmail.
